provenance.source_corpus: Limiting factors for lodgepole pine (Pinus contorta) and white spruce (Picea glauca) growth differ in some reconstructed sites in the Athabasca oil sands region

edges.original_sentence: The growth of lodgelole pine on sites reconstructed using the PMM as a capping soil over the TS substrate was mainly affected by water availability, while N availability, salinity and soil compaction combined limited Sw growth on reconstructed sites using the PMM over the OB substrate

edges.original_sentence: The VWC and dissolved organic carbon (DOC) together explained 80% of the variation in both the current annual increment of height (HI) (p = 0.007) and aboveground biomass (ABI) (p = 0.008)
